scope: Frontier efficiency and systems techniques relevant to long-context reasoning, memory bandwidth, sparse sequence processing, and efficient vision backbones.
source_policy: Primary sources only: official papers, official repositories, official project/model pages.

entries.title: PagedAttention / Paged KV Cache
entries.core_trick: Treat KV cache like a virtual-memory problem so long-context serving stays memory efficient and high throughput under variable sequence lengths.
entries.intuition: KV caching matters when serving or agent memory is the bottleneck; it is not a direct perception-model trick unless the control plane itself is the thing starving the loop.
apply_when: the research control plane is long-context and cache-heavy
you need many concurrent reasoning jobs without wasting memory
avoid_when: the active bottleneck is the perception model itself rather than the reasoning/control plane
the context sizes are small enough that paging adds complexity without benefit
ablation_nuggets: The main gain is systems efficiency under realistic multi-request loads, not a better model.
integration_pattern: apply to the long-running planner/critic stack, not the BEV student
failure_modes: confusing serving wins with model-quality wins
building cache complexity before the retrieval stack is healthy
tsqbev_actions: treat paged KV cache as a future supervisor-serving upgrade, not a perception-side experiment

entries.title: Multi-Head Latent Attention
entries.core_trick: Compress attention state into a latent space so inference retains strong reasoning quality while reducing KV-cache footprint and memory traffic.
entries.intuition: MLA is valuable when bandwidth and cache size dominate; it is a control-plane and large-model serving trick first, and only a perception trick if you explicitly redesign the camera token path around it.
apply_when: the reasoning stack or a very large teacher model is memory-bandwidth-bound
you want a latent interface instead of full-rank attention state
avoid_when: the local student is small enough that plain SDPA is not the bottleneck
you have not yet proven the basic feature path and are only adding complexity
ablation_nuggets: The point is state compression without catastrophic quality loss, not generic sparse attention.
integration_pattern: use MLA on the control plane or very large teachers before trying to transplant it into the local student
failure_modes: prematurely rewriting the perception stack around a serving optimization
ignoring specialized kernels needed to realize the gain
entries.student_role: experimental only after camera branch is stable
tsqbev_actions: prioritize MLA for the research control plane and large-teacher serving, not as the first student-side rewrite

entries.title: FlashMLA
entries.core_trick: Provide specialized kernels that make MLA fast enough in practice instead of leaving it as a paper-side algorithmic win.
entries.intuition: Latent attention without the right kernel is an idea; FlashMLA is the systems half that makes the idea real.
apply_when: MLA is already chosen and runtime kernel efficiency is the next blocker
avoid_when: the local hardware does not support the required kernel stack
the model is still too small to justify MLA complexity
ablation_nuggets: Kernel quality is often the difference between a promising attention method and a real speedup.
integration_pattern: treat FlashMLA as a serving/kernel optimization layer after MLA is justified
failure_modes: overinvesting in custom kernels before the retrieval/planner stack is architecturally stable
tsqbev_actions: use FlashMLA only if the planner/critic or a giant teacher actually moves to MLA

entries.title: State Space Models
entries.primary_bottleneck: quadratic attention cost on long sequences
entries.core_trick: Replace or reduce quadratic attention with selective state-space recurrence so sequence cost scales more gracefully while retaining long-range conditioning.
entries.intuition: SSMs are a scaling and systems play first. They become a vision or perception win only when long token sequences are truly the cost bottleneck and spatial inductive structure is preserved.
apply_when: sequence length dominates runtime and attention is the limiting factor
you can preserve spatial or geometric inductive structure around the SSM blocks
avoid_when: the main issue is weak supervision rather than sequence cost
short or moderate sequences already fit comfortably under efficient attention kernels
ablation_nuggets: The key win is scaling behavior; naive swaps often lose local structure and erase the benefit.
integration_pattern: evaluate SSMs as a later efficient student family or control-plane backbone, not as the first frontier teacher branch
failure_modes: rewriting the stack around SSMs before proving the teacher-supervision path
tsqbev_actions: keep SSMs as a later student-family branch after the DINOv3 plus teacher-distillation path is fully evaluated

entries.title: Vision Mamba
entries.core_trick: Use selective state-space sequence modeling to reduce attention-style scaling while preserving long-range dependency handling in vision backbones.
entries.intuition: Vision SSMs are attractive when sequence length hurts attention, but they need strong spatial inductive handling to avoid losing local geometric fidelity.
apply_when: image or BEV token length is the dominant bottleneck and attention is too expensive
you want a candidate efficient camera backbone family to test against transformer baselines
avoid_when: the active gap is teacher signal quality rather than camera backbone efficiency
the deployment stack already handles current attention costs well
ablation_nuggets: SSM gains depend heavily on how spatial structure is preserved.
Replacing attention blindly is not the point; the value is scaling behavior with the right inductive fixes.
integration_pattern: treat Vision Mamba as a later student-backbone branch, not the immediate frontier teacher path
failure_modes: rewriting the stack before teacher supervision is strong enough
entries.teacher_role: unlikely; not the strongest teacher default
tsqbev_actions: keep Vision Mamba as a later camera-student candidate, not the first frontier branch

entries.title: Mixture of Experts
entries.core_trick: Scale total parameter capacity by routing tokens to sparse experts so each token only pays for a small subset of the full model.
entries.intuition: MoE is compelling when the teacher needs much more capacity than a dense model can cheaply provide. It is usually the wrong first move for a latency-predictable edge student.
apply_when: teacher-side capacity is the bottleneck and infrastructure can handle sparse routing complexity
avoid_when: the final student must be predictable on edge hardware
small-batch serving makes routing overhead dominate
ablation_nuggets: MoE wins on capacity-per-token but adds load balancing and routing complexity that must be paid somewhere.
integration_pattern: treat generic MoE as a teacher-side scaling option only; distill back into a dense student
tsqbev_actions: if a much larger teacher is needed later, prefer MoE teacher experiments over MoE student deployment

entries.title: V-MoE
entries.primary_bottleneck: capacity scaling under limited per-token compute
entries.core_trick: Scale model capacity with sparsely activated experts so per-token compute stays manageable while total representational capacity grows.
entries.intuition: MoE belongs in the teacher much earlier than it belongs in the deployable student; routing complexity is real and batch-size sensitivity is harsh.
apply_when: teacher-side capacity needs to scale far beyond what dense models allow
avoid_when: the final student must be simple and latency-predictable on edge hardware
small-batch deployment makes expert routing overhead dominant
ablation_nuggets: MoE can buy capacity efficiently but adds routing, balance, and systems complexity.
It is usually teacher-first for constrained deployment settings.
integration_pattern: treat MoE as a teacher-only or offline frontier branch unless edge deployment constraints relax dramatically
failure_modes: routing overhead erasing the conditional-compute benefit
entries.student_role: usually avoid in the first deployable student
tsqbev_actions: reserve MoE for teacher-side scaling experiments and keep the edge student dense for now
